channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: PSG wins back-to-back Champions League titles after shootout victory against Arsenal
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain has won back-to-back Champions League titles by beating Arsenal on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest.
item.content:encoded:
Paris Saint-Germain has won back-to-back Champions League titles by beating Arsenal on penalties in a dramatic final in Budapest.

(Image credit: Denes Erdos)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says a ceasefire extension deal with Iran is near, but core issues remain
item.description: President Trump has not yet decided whether he'll extend a ceasefire with Iran, and Israel continues to attack targets in Lebanon, in spite of a ceasefire there.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has not yet decided whether he'll extend a ceasefire with Iran, and Israel continues to attack targets in Lebanon, in spite of a ceasefire there.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Over a thousand Palestinians from Gaza held in Israeli detention without charge
item.description: A controversial law allows Israel to hold Palestinians in prison without charge or trial. Israel says it's a necessary for security, but rights groups say it leaves detainees in a legal limbo.
item.content:encoded: A controversial law allows Israel to hold Palestinians in prison without charge or trial. Israel says it's a necessary for security, but rights groups say it leaves detainees in a legal limbo.
item.dc:creator: Itay Stern

item.title: Carcass of Timmy the humpback whale brought to shore in Denmark
item.description: The humpback whale, nicknamed "Timmy" by German media, died following a controversial failed rescue effort. His carcass had been drifting near the Danish shore for two weeks.
item.content:encoded:
The humpback whale, nicknamed "Timmy" by German media, died following a controversial failed rescue effort. His carcass had been drifting near the Danish shore for two weeks.

(Image credit: Philip Dulian)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hegseth urges Asian leaders to boost military spending against China
item.description: The U.S. defense secretary said there is "rightful alarm" of China's military build up. But he also struck a more moderate tone on U.S.-China relations – and notably sidestepped bringing up Taiwan.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. defense secretary said there is "rightful alarm" of China's military build up. But he also struck a more moderate tone on U.S.-China relations – and notably sidestepped bringing up Taiwan.

(Image credit: Anupam Nath)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: U.S. Embassy in India launches Trump-themed rickshaws to mark America's 250th birthday
item.description: Delhi's rickshaws are plying the roads with a new message: "HAPPY BIRTHDAY AMERICA" emblazoned with Trump's face. Drivers were offered sweeteners to host the signs: tea bags, soda, and an empty plastic bottle.
item.content:encoded: Delhi's rickshaws are plying the roads with a new message: "HAPPY BIRTHDAY AMERICA" emblazoned with Trump's face. Drivers were offered sweeteners to host the signs: tea bags, soda, and an empty plastic bottle.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Colombia's untapped wonder: The Mavecure Mountains
item.description: Far from Colombia's tourist hubs, the Mavecure Mountains rise from the Amazon jungle. Once off-limits during conflict, they now draw adventurous visitors to rare wildlife, sacred sites and vast views.
item.content:encoded:
Far from Colombia's tourist hubs, the Mavecure Mountains rise from the Amazon jungle. Once off-limits during conflict, they now draw adventurous visitors to rare wildlife, sacred sites and vast views.

(Image credit: John Otis)
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Myanmar's Min Aung Hlaing takes first foreign tour as leader, with visit to India
item.description: The tour comes as Myanmar's new government tries to consolidate its political position regionally, while continuing to wage a brutal civil war.
item.content:encoded:
The tour comes as Myanmar's new government tries to consolidate its political position regionally, while continuing to wage a brutal civil war.

(Image credit: Aung Shine Oo)
item.dc:creator: Michael Sullivan

item.title: Drones are changing the face of warfare, including battlefield medicine
item.description: There have been hundreds of thousands of casualties on both sides of the war in Ukraine, and by some estimates more than 80% are now caused by drones. It's changed the nature of battlefield medicine.
item.content:encoded: There have been hundreds of thousands of casualties on both sides of the war in Ukraine, and by some estimates more than 80% are now caused by drones. It's changed the nature of battlefield medicine.
item.dc:creator: Jay Price

item.title: Asia defense summit opens amid doubts over U.S. priorities
item.description: The Shangri-La Dialogue, hosted by the International Institute for Strategic Studies, will also address tensions in the Middle East and Russia's war on Ukraine.
item.content:encoded:
The Shangri-La Dialogue, hosted by the International Institute for Strategic Studies, will also address tensions in the Middle East and Russia's war on Ukraine.

(Image credit: Achmad Ibrahim)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
